translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Song of Songs, that [is] Solomon's.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let him kiss me with kisses of his mouth, For better [are] thy loves than wine.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For fragrance [are] thy perfumes good. Perfume emptied out -- thy name, Therefore have virgins loved thee!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Draw me: after thee we run, The king hath brought me into his inner chambers, We do joy and rejoice in thee, We mention thy loves more than wine, Uprightly they have loved thee!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dark [am] I, and comely, daughters of Jerusalem, As tents of Kedar, as curtains of Solomon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fear me not, because I [am] very dark, Because the sun hath scorched me, The sons of my mother were angry with me, They made me keeper of the vineyards, My vineyard -- my own -- I have not kept.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Declare to me, thou whom my soul hath loved, Where thou delightest, Where thou liest down at noon, For why am I as one veiled, By the ranks of thy companions?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If thou knowest not, O fair among women, Get thee forth by the traces of the flock, And feed thy kids by the shepherds' dwellings!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To my joyous one in chariots of Pharaoh, I have compared thee, my friend,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Comely have been thy cheeks with garlands, Thy neck with chains.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Garlands of gold we do make for thee, With studs of silver!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: While the king [is] in his circle, My spikenard hath given its fragrance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A bundle of myrrh [is] my beloved to me, Between my breasts it lodgeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A cluster of cypress [is] my beloved to me, In the vineyards of En-Gedi!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, thou [art] fair, my friend, Lo, thou [art] fair, thine eyes [are] doves!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, thou [art] fair, my love, yea, pleasant, Yea, our couch [is] green,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The beams of our houses [are] cedars, Our rafters [are] firs, I [am] a rose of Sharon, a lily of the valleys!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a lily among the thorns,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So [is] my friend among the daughters!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a citron among trees of the forest, So [is] my beloved among the sons, In his shade I delighted, and sat down, And his fruit [is] sweet to my palate.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath brought me in unto a house of wine, And his banner over me [is] love,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sustain me with grape-cakes, Support me with citrons, for I [am] sick with love.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His left hand [is] under my head, And his right doth embrace me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have adjured you, daughters of Jerusalem, By the roes or by the hinds of the field, Stir not up nor wake the love till she please!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The voice of my beloved! lo, this -- he is coming, Leaping on the mountains, skipping on the hills.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My beloved [is] like to a roe, Or to a young one of the harts. Lo, this -- he is standing behind our wall, Looking from the windows, Blooming from the lattice.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My beloved hath answered and said to me, 'Rise up, my friend, my fair one, and come away,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For lo, the winter hath passed by, The rain hath passed away -- it hath gone.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The flowers have appeared in the earth, The time of the singing hath come, And the voice of the turtle was heard in our land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The fig-tree hath ripened her green figs, And the sweet-smelling vines have given forth fragrance, Rise, come, my friend, my fair one, yea, come away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My dove, in clefts of the rock, In a secret place of the ascent, Cause me to see thine appearance, Cause me to hear thy voice, For thy voice [is] sweet, and thy appearance comely.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Seize ye for us foxes, Little foxes -- destroyers of vineyards, Even our sweet-smelling vineyards.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My beloved [is] mine, and I [am] his, Who is delighting among the lilies,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till the day doth break forth, And the shadows have fled away, Turn, be like, my beloved, To a roe, or to a young one of the harts, On the mountains of separation!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On my couch by night, I sought him whom my soul hath loved; I sought him, and I found him not!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: -- Pray, let me rise, and go round the city, In the streets and in the broad places, I seek him whom my soul hath loved! -- I sought him, and I found him not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The watchmen have found me, (Who are going round about the city), 'Him whom my soul have loved saw ye?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But a little I passed on from them, Till I found him whom my soul hath loved! I seized him, and let him not go, Till I brought him in unto the house of my mother -- And the chamber of her that conceived me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have adjured you, daughters of Jerusalem, By the roes or by the hinds of the field, Stir not up nor wake the love till she please!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who [is] this coming up from the wilderness, Like palm-trees of smoke, Perfumed [with] myrrh and frankincense, From every powder of the merchant?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, his couch, that [is] Solomon's, Sixty mighty ones [are] around it, Of the mighty of Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All of them holding sword, taught of battle, Each his sword by his thigh, for fear at night.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A palanquin king Solomon made for himself, Of the wood of Lebanon,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Its pillars he made of silver, Its bottom of gold, its seat of purple, Its midst lined [with] love, By the daughters of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Go forth, and look, ye daughters of Zion, On king Solomon, with the crown, With which his mother crowned him, In the day of his espousals, And in the day of the joy of his heart!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, thou [art] fair, my friend, lo, thou [art] fair, Thine eyes [are] doves behind thy veil, Thy hair as a row of the goats That have shone from mount Gilead,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy teeth as a row of the shorn ones That have come up from the washing, For all of them are forming twins, And a bereaved one is not among them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a thread of scarlet [are] thy lips, And thy speech [is] comely, As the work of the pomegranate [is] thy temple behind thy veil,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As the tower of David [is] thy neck, built for an armoury, The chief of the shields are hung on it, All shields of the mighty.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy two breasts [are] as two fawns, Twins of a roe, that are feeding among lilies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till the day doth break forth, And the shadows have fled away, I will get me unto the mountain of myrrh, And unto the hill of frankincense.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou [art] all fair, my friend, And a blemish there is not in thee. Come from Lebanon, O spouse,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Come from Lebanon, come thou in. Look from the top of Amana, From the top of Shenir and Hermon, From the habitations of lions, From the mountains of leopards.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast emboldened me, my sister-spouse, Emboldened me with one of thine eyes, With one chain of thy neck.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How wonderful have been thy loves, my sister-spouse, How much better have been thy loves than wine, And the fragrance of thy perfumes than all spices.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy lips drop honey, O spouse, Honey and milk [are] under thy tongue, And the fragrance of thy garments [Is] as the fragrance of Lebanon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A garden shut up [is] my sister-spouse, A spring shut up -- a fountain sealed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy shoots a paradise of pomegranates, With precious fruits,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cypresses with nard -- nard and saffron, Cane and cinnamon, With all trees of frankincense, Myrrh and aloes, with all chief spices.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A fount of gardens, a well of living waters, And flowings from Lebanon!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Awake, O north wind, and come, O south, Cause my garden to breathe forth, its spices let flow, Let my beloved come to his garden, And eat its pleasant fruits!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have come in to my garden, my sister-spouse, I have plucked my myrrh with my spice, I have eaten my comb with my honey, I have drunk my wine with my milk. Eat, O friends, drink, Yea, drink abundantly, O beloved ones!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I am sleeping, but my heart waketh: The sound of my beloved knocking! 'Open to me, my sister, my friend, My dove, my perfect one, For my head is filled [with] dew, My locks [with] drops of the night.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have put off my coat, how do I put it on? I have washed my feet, how do I defile them?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My beloved sent his hand from the net-work, And my bowels were moved for him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I rose to open to my beloved, And my hands dropped myrrh, Yea, my fingers flowing myrrh, On the handles of the lock.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I opened to my beloved, But my beloved withdrew -- he passed on, My soul went forth when he spake, I sought him, and found him not. I called him, and he answered me not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The watchmen who go round about the city, Found me, smote me, wounded me, Keepers of the walls lifted up my veil from off me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have adjured you, daughters of Jerusalem, If ye find my beloved -- What do ye tell him? that I [am] sick with love!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What [is] thy beloved above [any] beloved, O fair among women? What [is] thy beloved above [any] beloved, That thus thou hast adjured us?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My beloved [is] clear and ruddy, Conspicuous above a myriad!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His head [is] pure gold -- fine gold, His locks flowing, dark as a raven,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His eyes as doves by streams of water, Washing in milk, sitting in fulness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His cheeks as a bed of the spice, towers of perfumes, His lips [are] lilies, dropping flowing myrrh,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His hands rings of gold, set with beryl, His heart bright ivory, covered with sapphires,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His limbs pillars of marble, Founded on sockets of fine gold, His appearance as Lebanon, choice as the cedars.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His mouth is sweetness -- and all of him desirable, This [is] my beloved, and this my friend, O daughters of Jerusalem!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whither hath thy beloved gone, O fair among women? Whither hath thy beloved turned, And we seek him with thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My beloved went down to his garden, To the beds of the spice, To delight himself in the gardens, and to gather lilies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I [am] my beloved's, and my beloved [is] mine, Who is delighting himself among the lilies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fair [art] thou, my friend, as Tirzah, Comely as Jerusalem, Awe-inspiring as bannered hosts.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn round thine eyes from before me, Because they have made me proud. Thy hair [is] as a row of the goats, That have shone from Gilead,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy teeth as a row of the lambs, That have come up from the washing, Because all of them are forming twins, And a bereaved one is not among them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As the work of the pomegranate [is] thy temple behind thy veil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sixty are queens, and eighty concubines, And virgins without number.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: One is my dove, my perfect one, One she [is] of her mother, The choice one she [is] of her that bare her, Daughters saw, and pronounce her happy, Queens and concubines, and they praise her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Who [is] this that is looking forth as morning, Fair as the moon -- clear as the sun, Awe-inspiring as bannered hosts?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Unto a garden of nuts I went down, To look on the buds of the valley, To see whither the vine had flourished, The pomegranates had blossomed --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I knew not my soul, It made me -- chariots of my people Nadib.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Return, return, O Shulammith! Return, return, and we look upon thee. What do ye see in Shulammith?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As the chorus of 'Mahanaim.' How beautiful were thy feet with sandals, O daughter of Nadib. The turnings of thy sides [are] as ornaments, Work of the hands of an artificer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy waist [is] a basin of roundness, It lacketh not the mixture, Thy body a heap of wheat, fenced with lilies,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy two breasts as two young ones, twins of a roe,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy neck as a tower of the ivory, Thine eyes pools in Heshbon, near the gate of Bath-Rabbim, Thy face as a tower of Lebanon looking to Damascus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy head upon thee as Carmel, And the locks of thy head as purple, The king is bound with the flowings!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How fair and how pleasant hast thou been, O love, in delights.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This thy stature hath been like to a palm, And thy breasts to clusters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I said, 'Let me go up on the palm, Let me lay hold on its boughs, Yea, let thy breasts be, I pray thee, as clusters of the vine, And the fragrance of thy face as citrons,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thy palate as the good wine -- 'Flowing to my beloved in uprightness, Strengthening the lips of the aged!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I [am] my beloved's, and on me [is] his desire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Come, my beloved, we go forth to the field,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: We lodge in the villages, we go early to the vineyards, We see if the vine hath flourished, The sweet smelling-flower hath opened. The pomegranates have blossomed, There do I give to thee my loves;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The mandrakes have given fragrance, And at our openings all pleasant things, New, yea, old, my beloved, I laid up for thee!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who doth make thee as a brother to me, Sucking the breasts of my mother? I find thee without, I kiss thee, Yea, they do not despise me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I lead thee, I bring thee in unto my mother's house, She doth teach me, I cause thee to drink of the perfumed wine, Of the juice of my pomegranate,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His left hand [is] under my head, And his right doth embrace me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have adjured you, daughters of Jerusalem, How ye stir up, And how ye wake the love till she please!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who [is] this coming from the wilderness, Hasting herself for her beloved? Under the citron-tree I have waked thee, There did thy mother pledge thee, There she gave a pledge [that] bare thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Set me as a seal on thy heart, as a seal on thine arm, For strong as death is love, Sharp as Sheol is jealousy, Its burnings [are] burnings of fire, a flame of Jah!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Many waters are not able to quench the love, And floods do not wash it away. If one give all the wealth of his house for love, Treading down -- they tread upon it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: We have a little sister, and breasts she hath not, What do we do for our sister, In the day that it is told of her?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If she is a wall, we build by her a palace of silver. And if she is a door, We fashion by her board-work of cedar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I [am] a wall, and my breasts as towers, Then I have been in his eyes as one finding peace.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Solomon hath a vineyard in Baal-Hamon, He hath given the vineyard to keepers, Each bringeth for its fruit a thousand silverlings;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My vineyard -- my own -- is before me, The thousand [is] for thee, O Solomon. And the two hundred for those keeping its fruit. O dweller in gardens!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The companions are attending to thy voice, Cause me to hear. Flee, my beloved, and be like to a roe,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Or to a young one of the harts on mountains of spices!
